mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, consumer spending capacity collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or walk-forward Sharpe ratio of the combined signal remains below 0.5 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in the index of industrial production reflect immediate capacity utilization improvements and supply chain stabilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are uncorrelated with INDPRO changes (Rank IC < 0.01) or the signal reverses direction during acute liquidity crunches.

mechanism_narrative: Low inflation combined with near-zero policy rates creates a real negative interest rate environment that forces portfolio rebalancing into equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain flat or negative despite negative real rates, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict equity direction (Rank IC < 0.02) or positive payroll surprises coincide with negative market returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP contractions exceeding 1.0% signal severe demand destruction and earnings recession risks, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in recession depth.
failure_condition: Markets rally on negative GDP prints due to policy expectations, causing the signal to predict negative returns when actual returns are positive in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable or declining federal funds rates indicates accommodative policy successfully stimulating real output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns when monetary policy tightens prematurely, or the interaction effect vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) during stagflationary periods.
